'''Jason Demetriou''' ({{IPAc-en|ˈ|d|ə|m|iː|t|r|ɪ|oʊ|}}) (born 13 January 1976) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] coach who is the head coach of the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]] and {{rlnt|Papua New Guinea}} at international level.

He is a former head coach of the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and a former professional rugby league footballer.

Predominantly a {{rlp|ce}} as a player, Demetriou spent all of his 13-year playing career in England, playing for [[Chorley Lynx|Lancashire Lynx]], [[Rochdale Hornets]], [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and the [[Keighley Cougars]] and for {{rlnt|Canada}} at international level. 

He has previously held head coaching jobs at the [[Keighley Cougars]], [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]] and the [[Illawarra Cutters]] and has been an assistant coach at the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]], [[Brisbane Broncos]] and [[North Queensland Cowboys]], where he was on the coaching staff that won the [[2015 NRL Grand Final]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Born in Sydney, Demetriou grew up in the [[St George (Sydney)|St George area]] of Sydney and supported the [[Balmain Tigers]] as a child. He is of [[Greeks|Greek]]<ref name="Greek influence">{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/03/25/greek-influence-strong-at-rabbitohs/ |title=Greek influence strong at Rabbitohs |publisher=NRL.com |first=Brad |last=Walter |date=25 March 2022}}</ref> and [[Canadians|Canadian]] descent; his grandmother was born in [[Alberta]].<ref name="23022024 SEN interview">{{cite episode|date=23 February 2024|title=South Sydney Coach Jason Demetriou on the teams preparations for Vegas|series=Breakfast with Vossy & Brandy|network=[[Sports Entertainment Network]]|url=https://app.podscribe.ai/episode/96830467|quote=Yeah, I did, Vossy, in the 2000 Emerging Nations in the World Cup over in England. I played twice for Canada in the three game series they had and yeah, my nan was born in Canada, in Alberta, so it was great to represent her side of the family and put on the Canadian jersey.}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=[[Canada Rugby League]]|date=2 March 2024|title=Instagram post|url=https://www.instagram.com/canada_rl/p/C3-bZ-TLUH2/|website=Instagram|access-date=29 March 2024}}</ref> As a teen, Demetriou played in the [[Harold Matthews Cup]] and [[S.G. Ball Cup]] for the [[St George Dragons]]. From there Demetriou moved to the [[Newtown Jets]], where he played for their [[Ron Massey Cup|Metropolitan Cup]] side and later the Bondi Roosters, before moving to England in December 1999. In 2000, Demetriou played for the now-defunct [[Chorley Lynx|Lancashire Lynx]] and the [[Rochdale Hornets]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://richarddelariviere.co.uk/?p=344 |title=Jason Demetriou {{!}} Richard de la Rivière |website=richarddelariviere.co.uk |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131021120603/http://richarddelariviere.co.uk/?p=344 |archive-date=2013-10-21}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings===
In 2001, Demetriou joined the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the second division [[Rugby Football League Championship Second Division|Northern Ford Premiership]], with side gaining promotion to the [[Super League]] in Demetriou's first season with club and Demetriou being named the 2001 Northern Ford Premiership Overseas Player of the Year.

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
In 2004, Demetriou joined the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] ([[Captain (sports)|captain]]) where he spent the next seven seasons, captaining the club for five of those seasons. In doing so, he became the first Australian player without [[National Rugby League|NRL]] experience to captain a [[Super League]] team.

In [[Super League XI|2006]], he was named Wakefield Trinity Wildcat's Player of the Year. That season he scored a late try against local rivals, the [[Castleford Tigers]], to keep Wakefield Trinity Wildcats in the [[Super League]], simultaneously relegating Castleford Tigers. The moment was captured and a large copy of the photo is on display outside the coach's office at [[Belle Vue (Wakefield)|Belle Vue]].<ref name="NEW STARS SIGN UP WITH THE VIKINGS">{{cite web|url=http://archive.runcornandwidnesworld.co.uk/2001/9/26/207927.html|title=NEW STARS SIGN UP WITH THE VIKINGS|publisher=Runcorn and Widnes World|date=26 September 2001|access-date=13 July 2007}}</ref>

In 2007, he was named as Wakefield Trinity Wildcats' Player of the Year again. He was also named at centre in the Super League Dream Team, and was short-listed for the [[Man of Steel award]] along with [[Trent Barrett]] and [[James Roby]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Whitelock |first=Adam |date=2020-05-17 |title=Demetriou's time in England |url=https://everythingrugbyleague.com/demetrious-time-in-england/ |access-date=2025-07-31 |website=Everything Rugby League |language=en-US}}</ref>
[[File:Jason Demetriou Wakey.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Demetriou playing for the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats in 2008]]
In [[Super League XIV|2009]], he wore the number 8 jersey for Wakefield Trinity Wildcats, in memory of his close friend [[Adam Watene]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Wilson |first=Andy |date=2008-12-17 |title=Wakefield announce tributes for Watene |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/dec/17/wakefieldtrinity-castleford |access-date=2025-07-31 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref> Demetriou is associated with the Adam Watene Fund, helping raise funds for his family in New Zealand. In [[Super League XV|2010]], he was awarded a 3-month testimonial at the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats as a reward for his hard work and dedication to English rugby league.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2010-03-30 |title=Demetriou wins testimonial honour |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wakefield/8595142.stm |access-date=2025-07-31 |language=en-GB}}</ref>

===International career===
Demetriou played two games for {{rlnt|Canada}} at the [[2000 Rugby League Emerging Nations Tournament]].<ref name="23022024 SEN interview" />

Demetriou said in 2022, "I should have played twice for {{rlnt|Greece}} before I retired but off-season surgery stopped me. Probably the one regret I had during my career was not being able to represent Greece."<ref name="Greek influence" />

==Coaching career==
===Keighley Cougars ===
Demetriou's contract with Wakefield Trinity Wildcats expired at the end of 2010, and he was told that it would not be renewed. He initially joined the [[York City Knights]] for 2011, but a subsequent offer as [[player-coach]] for [[Keighley Cougars]] arose, and was accepted. In his first season in charge, Keighley gained promotion to the [[Championship (rugby league)|Kingstone Press Championship]].

===Northern Pride===
Demetriou returned to Australia in 2013, becoming the head coach for the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. During his two seasons at the Pride, the club won back-to-back minor premierships, the 2014 Queensland Cup Grand Final and the inaugural [[NRL State Championship]] game. In 2014, he coached the Queensland Residents side, who defeated the [[NSW Cup]] rep side 24–16.

===North Queensland Cowboys===
In October 2014, Demetriou joined the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] as an assistant coach.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2014/10/15/demetriou_to_join_coaching_team.html|title=Demetriou to join coaching team|date=14 October 2014}}</ref> On 4 October 2015, Demetriou was a member of the North Queensland coaching staff in the side's 17–16 [[2015 NRL Grand Final|Grand Final]] victory over [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].

===St George Dragons===
In January 2016, Demetriou joined the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] as an assistant coach and as head coach to their [[NSW Cup]] feeder team, the [[Illawarra Cutters]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/news/2016/01/08/dragons_announce_key.html|title = Dragons Announce Key Staff Changes|date = 7 January 2016}}</ref> In September 2016, he was named coach of the 2016 [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|author=NSWRL.com.au|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/2016-intrust-super-cup-team-of-the-year-23923/|title=2016 Intrust Super Cup Team of the Year|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2016-03-26|access-date=2016-09-23}}</ref> He went on to win the grand final with the Cutters that year.

===Brisbane Broncos===
In 2017, Demetriou joined [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]] under head coach [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2016/10/28/jason-demetriou-joins-broncos-staff/|title = Jason Demetriou Joins Broncos' Staff|date = 27 October 2016}}</ref> He took over as the side's attacking coach in Round 6 of the [[2018 NRL season]], with the side scoring the second most tries in the competition from that point forward.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/08/16/why-broncos-assistant-jason-demetriou-has-what-it-takes-to-be-an-nrl-head-coach/|title = Why Broncos assistant Demetriou has what it takes to be an NRL head coach|date = 15 August 2018}}</ref>

===South Sydney Rabbitohs===
In December 2018, he joined [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] as an assistant coach, following Bennett to the club.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2018/12/13/rabbitohs-announce-support-staff/|title=Rabbitohs Announce Support Staff|date=13 December 2018}}</ref> In February 2020, it was announced that Demetriou would succeed Bennett as head coach at the end of the 2021 NRL season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-wayne-bennett-successor-contract-jason-demetriou-south-sydney-rabbitohs-rugby-league/news-story/a27dcd593de99039dc23ecde6a5c1e7d|title=Bennett's reign to end in 2021 as Souths lock in successor. But Wayne is eyeing another gig|publisher=Fox Sports Australia|date=21 February 2020|access-date=21 February 2020}}</ref> On 6 August 2020, it was announced Demetriou would make his coaching debut for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] after head coach Wayne Bennett breached the NRL's COVID-19 protocols.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-wayne-bennett-covid19-bubble-breach-south-sydney-rabbitohs/news-story/70581f9cc04e59546b30dfd8394ea0da|title = 'Jeopardises the game': Bennett gets brutal COVID 'wake up call' from ARLC boss|date = 6 August 2020}}</ref> 
[[File:Jason Demetriou London Broncos January 2026.jpg|thumb|right|Demetriou down at the touch line at Wimbledon in January 2026]]
His first match as coach saw the South Sydney club defeat [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]] by 28–10 at [[Stadium Australia|ANZ Stadium]] in Sydney.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-08-07/nrl-brisbane-loses-to-south-sydney-28-10-warriors-beat-manly/12536880|title=South Sydney beats Brisbane Broncos 28-10 in NRL grudge match, Manly loses to Warriors|publisher=ABC News ([[Australian Broadcasting Corporation]])|date=7 August 2020|access-date=7 August 2020}}</ref>

Following the conclusion of the [[2021 NRL season]], he replaced the outgoing Bennett as the new head coach of South Sydney.  In his first official game in charge, South Sydney were defeated 11-4 by [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]] at [[Suncorp Stadium]].  In round 3 of the [[2022 NRL season]], he earned his first win in charge of South Sydney against arch rivals the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/03/11/broncos-stun-rabbitohs-as-capewell-haas-stand-tall/|title=Broncos stun Rabbitohs as Capewell, Haas stand tall|website=www.nrl.com|date=11 March 2022 }}</ref>
In his first full season at South Sydney, he guided the club to their fifth straight preliminary final.  Souths took an early 12-0 lead over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] but were eventually defeated 32-12.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-09-24/nrl-penrith-panthers-defeats-south-sydney-rabbitohs/101470088/|title=Penrith Panthers defeat South Sydney Rabbitohs 32-12 to qualify for third straight NRL grand final|newspaper=ABC News|date=24 September 2022 }}</ref>
In his second season at the club, Demetriou guided Souths to second place on the table during the midway point of the [[2023 NRL season]]. However, the club would suffer a form slump in the second half of the year only winning four matches. South Sydney would finish 9th on the table missing the finals for the first time since 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-south-sydney-rabbitohs-latrell-mitchell-changing-position-centres-halves-fullback-jack-wighton-sam-burgess-2024-jason-demetriou/news-story/d7c72c901f9e61a0b76b55f9162b64cd/|title=‘Absolute madness’: Potential Latrell switch slammed as a ‘band aid’ for broken Bunnies|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

At the start of the [[2024 NRL season]] under Demetriou, South Sydney lost their opening three matches of the season including a humiliating 48-6 loss against arch-rivals the Sydney Roosters. It was South Sydney's worst start to a season since 2008.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/news/nrl-2024-sydney-roosters-v-south-sydney-rabbitohs-live-blog-teams-video-jason-demetriou-lachlan-ilias-dean-hawkins/news-story/c45c44656145dd4aa519f97de60f713a/|title=‘Total domination’: Dismal Souths could hit 14-year-low amid horror rout — Big hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>
Ahead of the clubs round 6 game against Cronulla, it was reported that Demetriou would be sacked as South Sydney head coach if they failed to win the match. Despite the team losing the match 34-22, Demetriou was not terminated due to an observable improvement in the team's effort for the game. The team would ultimately suffer yet another defeat after their subsequent bye, losing to [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]] 54-20. Although no formal announcement was made by the club about Demetriou's status immediately following the loss, it was reported that club officials would be holding an official meeting regarding his future.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-news-2024-jason-demetriou-coaching-future-mal-meninga-linked-to-south-sydney-rabbitohs-interim-coach/news-story/f02e28516c4b023cc86246c096edf57c/|title=‘Shouldn’t get involved with that club’: The ‘red flag’ that worries Mal about potential Souths job|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref> On 30 April 2024, the club formally terminated Demetriou as coach, appointing [[Ben Hornby]] as an interim for the remainder of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/blog/2024/apr/30/jason-demetriou-oversaw-south-sydneys-steep-decline-his-sacking-can-start-the-reversal/|title=Jason Demetriou oversaw South Sydney’s steep decline. His sacking can start the reversal|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Papua New Guinea ===
On 27 August 2024, it was reported that Demetriou had been appointed coach of the men's [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2024/08/27/demetriou-to-take-charge-of-kumuls/|title=Demetriou to take charge of Kumuls|last=Walter|first=Brad|date=27 Aug 2024|website=NRL|access-date=27 Aug 2024}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
In September 2025, Demetriou was confirmed as the new head coach of the [[London Broncos]] from 2026 onwards. This was confirmed to be a three-year deal after the [[Darren Lockyer]] led takeover of the London side was ratified in September 2025.<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm2z353l45eo Lockyer sees 'exciting opportunity' to buy Broncos]</ref><ref>[https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13431669/london-broncos-super-league-bid-for-2026-expansion-confirmed-as-jason-demetriou-commits-to-club London Broncos: Super League bid for 2026 expansion confirmed as Jason Demetriou 'commits' to club]</ref><ref>[https://www.totalrl.com/london-broncos-secure-jason-demetriou-on-three-year-pre-commitment-as-new-owners-confirmed/ London Broncos secure Jason Demetriou on “three-year pre-commitment” as new owners confirmed]</ref><ref>[https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/darren-lockyer-london-broncos-plan-1380002 Darren Lockyer confirms Jason Demetriou and major NRL signings in London Broncos plan]</ref><ref>[https://www.msn.com/en-au/sport/rugby_league/darren-lockyer-moves-to-purchase-london-broncos-identifies-demetriou-as-coach/ar-AA1LPAnM?cvid=5bfb22c9f083450981df6b42cfb02976&ocid=hpmsn&apiversion=v2&noservercache=1&domshim=1&renderwebcomponents=1&wcseo=1&batchservertelemetry=1&noservertelemetry=1 Darren Lockyer moves to purchase London Broncos, identifies Demetriou as coach]</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c5y8km8g7gyo/|title=Demetriou to join London Broncos as head coach|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
